Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.controller.GameController
All Implemented Interfaces:
ITimerListener
public class GameController extends Object implements ITimerListener
Controller for the main game view (GameView.fxml).

Responsibilities:

  • Receiving the machine count from the selection screen via initData(int).
  • Setting up the UI layout according to the number of players.
  • Rendering each player's hand and the table state.
  • Handling human player card clicks and deck clicks.
  • Delegating computer turns to Game triggered by GameTimer callbacks.
  • Showing elimination and game-over messages via the overlay.

This controller implements ITimerListener so it receives timer callbacks directly. All UI updates inside callbacks are wrapped in Platform.runLater() since they arrive from background threads.

See Also:
  • Game
  • GameTimer

    Registers keyboard shortcuts for the human player's turn: 1â€“4 to highlight a card, Enter to play the selected card, T to draw from the deck.

    Shows only the machine zones needed for this game configuration.

    Updates the sum label CSS class based on proximity to 50.

    • GameController

      public GameController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()

    • initData

      public void initData(int machineCount)
      Receives the machine count from the selection screen, creates the Game and GameTimer, sets up the layout, deals the initial hands, and starts the general timer.
      Parameters:
      machineCount - number of computer players (1â€“3)

    • setupLayout

      private void setupLayout(int machineCount)
      Shows only the machine zones needed for this game configuration.
      Parameters:
      machineCount - number of computer players (1â€“3)

    • startTurn

      private void startTurn()
      Starts the current player's turn: checks for automatic elimination, updates the turn indicator, and either enables human interaction or triggers the machine timer.

    • onHumanCardClicked

      private void onHumanCardClicked(int cardIndex)
      Handles a click on one of the human player's cards. Plays the card if legal, then renders the updated state.
      Parameters:
      cardIndex - the index of the clicked card in the hand

    • onDeckClicked

      private void onDeckClicked()
      Handles a click on the deck â€” the human draws a replacement card after having played one this turn.

    • onPlayAgain

      private void onPlayAgain()
      Called by the message overlay button â€” restarts the game with the same number of machine players.

    • onTick

      public void onTick(int elapsedSeconds)
      Called every second by the general game timer thread.

      Use this to update the elapsed-time label in the UI.

      Updates the elapsed-time label every second on the JavaFX thread.
      Specified by:
      onTick in interface ITimerListener
      Parameters:
      elapsedSeconds - total seconds elapsed since the game started

    • onMachineTurnReady

      public void onMachineTurnReady()
      Called when the machine turn delay (2â€“4 s) has elapsed and the computer player is ready to play a card.

      The controller should respond by calling on the JavaFX thread.

      The machine plays its card when the thinking delay has elapsed.
      Specified by:
      onMachineTurnReady in interface ITimerListener

    • onMachineDrawReady

      public void onMachineDrawReady()
      Called when the machine draw delay (1â€“2 s) has elapsed and the computer player is ready to draw a replacement card.

      The controller should respond by calling on the JavaFX thread.

      The machine draws a replacement card, then the turn advances.
      Specified by:
      onMachineDrawReady in interface ITimerListener

    • renderAll

      private void renderAll()
      Re-renders all UI components to reflect the current model state.

    • renderTable

      private void renderTable()
      Updates the table pane with the current top card image and the sum label.

    • renderDeck

      private void renderDeck()
      Updates the deck pane and the remaining card count label.

    • renderMachineHands

      private void renderMachineHands()
      Re-renders all machine player hands (cards face down).

    • renderMachineHand

      private void renderMachineHand(javafx.scene.layout.Pane box, IPlayer player)

    • renderHumanHand

      private void renderHumanHand(boolean clickable)
      Re-renders the human player's hand.
      Parameters:
      clickable - whether the cards should respond to mouse clicks

    • createCardView

      private javafx.scene.image.ImageView createCardView(Card card, boolean faceUp, int index, boolean clickable)
      Creates an ImageView for the given card.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to render
      faceUp - whether to show the front or back image
      index - hand index used for click handler (-1 to skip)
      clickable - whether to attach a mouse click handler
      Returns:
      the configured ImageView

    • handleGameOver

      private void handleGameOver()
      Handles the end of the game by stopping the timers and showing the winner overlay.

    • showMessage

      private void showMessage(String message, boolean showButton)
      Displays the message overlay with the given text.
      Parameters:
      message - the message to display
      showButton - whether to show the "Play again" button

    • loadImage

      private javafx.scene.image.Image loadImage(String filename)
      Loads an image from the resources images directory.
      Parameters:
      filename - the image filename (e.g. "ace_of_spades.png")
      Returns:
      the loaded Image

    • formatTime

      private String formatTime(int totalSeconds)
      Formats elapsed seconds as MM:SS.
      Parameters:
      totalSeconds - total seconds elapsed
      Returns:
      formatted time string

    • updateSumStyle

      private void updateSumStyle()
      Updates the sum label CSS class based on proximity to 50. Normal â†’ warning (â‰¥40) â†’ danger (â‰¥47).

    • flashSumLabel

      private void flashSumLabel()
      Briefly flashes the sum label to give visual feedback when the human tries to play an illegal card.

    • showAceOverlay

      private void showAceOverlay()
      Displays the Ace value selection overlay, allowing the human player to choose between adding 1 or 10 to the table sum.

      The +10 button is automatically disabled if adding 10 would cause the table sum to exceed 50.

    • onAceOne

      private void onAceOne()
      Handles the human player's choice of Ace value 1. Hides the overlay, plays the Ace with value 1, and resets the pending Ace index.

    • onAceTen

      private void onAceTen()
      Handles the human player's choice of Ace value 10. Hides the overlay, plays the Ace with value 10, and resets the pending Ace index.

    • playHumanCardWithAceValue

      private void playHumanCardWithAceValue(int cardIndex, int chosenValue)
      Plays the Ace card at the given hand index with the human-chosen value, bypassing the automatic value selection in Card.getValue(int).

      The card is removed from the hand, sent to the discard pile, and the table sum is updated directly via Table.addToSum(int) with the chosen value.

      Parameters:
      cardIndex - the index of the Ace in the human player's hand (0â€“3)
      chosenValue - the value chosen by the human player (1 or 10)

    • playHumanCard

      private void playHumanCard(int cardIndex)
      Plays a non-Ace card from the human player's hand at the given index.

      Delegates to Game.humanPlayCard(int) and updates the UI. Shows a visual flash on the sum label if the card is not legally playable.

      Parameters:
      cardIndex - the index of the card in the human player's hand (0â€“3)

    • highlightActivePlayer

      private void highlightActivePlayer(IPlayer current)
      Highlights the active player's zone with a golden border and full card opacity, while dimming all other players' hands to 0.35 opacity.

      Called at the start of every turn from startTurn().

      Parameters:
      current - the IPlayer whose turn it currently is

    • setHandOpacity

      private void setHandOpacity(javafx.scene.layout.Pane box, double opacity)
      Sets the opacity of all card nodes inside the given container.

      Used by highlightActivePlayer(IPlayer) to dim or restore the visibility of a player's hand.

      Parameters:
      box - the Pane containing the card nodes
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (invisible) and 1.0 (fully visible)

    • onGoHome

      private void onGoHome()
      Navigates back to the welcome screen. Stops all timers before leaving the game screen.

    • setupKeyboardShortcuts

      private void setupKeyboardShortcuts()
      Registers keyboard shortcuts for the human player's turn: 1â€“4 to highlight a card, Enter to play the selected card, T to draw from the deck.

    • selectCard

      private void selectCard(int index)
      Highlights the card at the given index as if the mouse hovered over it, removing the highlight from any previously selected card.
      Parameters:
      index - the hand index (0â€“3) to select

    • playSelectedCard

      private void playSelectedCard()
      Plays the currently keyboard-selected card, equivalent to clicking it.
